title: Server-as-compiler: the aion-toolchain authoring loop
purpose: Build the designed-but-absent server-side authoring loop (RM-026): submit Gleam source, the server type-checks it, returns errors inline, packages, and hot-loads — an authoring REPL against a running engine with no local toolchain. An asset only an engine that owns a hot-loading VM can offer, and the foundation for a hosted playground.
task: Make authoring a live loop against the running engine, optionally. In: a new aion-toolchain crate that compiles and type-checks Gleam workflow source by shelling out to the gleam binary and packages a .aion on success; and aion-server authoring endpoints, gated on --gleam-path, that accept source, return the gleam type error inline on failure, and package and hot-load on success. Out: embedding the Gleam compiler (the toolchain always shells out); making the toolchain mandatory (without --gleam-path the server deploys pre-built .aion only).

requirements.title: aion-toolchain compiles, type-checks, and packages
requirements.spec: WHEN given Gleam workflow source, THE SYSTEM SHALL compile and type-check it by shelling out to the gleam binary and package a .aion on success. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT embed a Gleam compiler.
acceptance: aion-toolchain compiles a valid workflow source to a loadable .aion by invoking the external gleam binary.
A type-erroneous source yields the gleam type error rather than a panic or a partial package.
The crate embeds no compiler — verified by its dependency set carrying no Gleam compiler crate.

requirements.title: Server authoring endpoints, gated and optional
requirements.spec: WHEN Gleam source is submitted to the authoring endpoint and --gleam-path is configured, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the gleam type error inline on a compile failure and package and hot-load on success. WHEN --gleam-path is absent, THE SYSTEM SHALL deploy pre-built .aion files only and carry no compiler dependency.
acceptance: Submitting a type-erroneous workflow returns the gleam error inline over the wire.
Submitting a corrected workflow packages it via aion-toolchain and hot-loads it so it runs.
Without --gleam-path the authoring endpoints are absent and the server still deploys pre-built .aion files (CN7).

boundaries: SHALL NOT embed the Gleam compiler in aion or aion-server — aion-toolchain shells out to the gleam binary.
SHALL NOT make server-side authoring mandatory — it is gated on --gleam-path and optional.
SHALL NOT add codegen (WA-001) or the dev loop (WA-002).
verification: Inline type-error reporting proven by submitting an erroneous then a corrected workflow.
Optionality proven: without --gleam-path the server has no compiler dependency and still deploys .aion.
